identity.canonical_title: Physical Consequences & Interpretation (What You Asked: Two-Arm Pull, Hemisph: Ψ_rm (1)

theory.canonical_statement: # 4) Physical consequences & interpretation (what you asked: two-arm pull, hemisphere-specific effects) - **Dipole arms**: \(\Psi_{\rm dipole}\propto\cos\theta\) produces opposite-sign circulation in the two hemispheres — a natural “diopole” where each pole acts like an arm of the vortex pulling its hemisphere inward. - **Coriolis modification**: because \(f=2\Omega\cos\theta\) changes sign across the equator, advection of absolute vorticity \(q=\zeta+f\) produces strong asymmetries near the equator — wave radiation and cross-equatorial mixing are suppressed or modified. The hemispheric arms therefore behave differently (e.g., different effective Rossby wave speeds). - **Rossby wave modulation**: the wave sum produces westward-drifting undulations on the dipole arms; certain \(l,m\) modes resonate with the dipole and can either strengthen an arm locally (constructive interference) or shred it (destructive). - **Point vortex / concentrated pull**: adding \(\Psi_{\rm vortex}\) near a pole creates a concentrated local pull that advects/steers the dipole arm in that hemisphere; if you place unequal \(\Gamma\) at the two poles you get hemispheric asymmetry. - **Winding & topology**: the streamfunction defines closed and open streamlines; the vorticity distribution and circulation around poles give a topological “winding number” if you follow streamlines around poles. Superposition of rotating dipole + wave modes can create spiral/Archimedean-like patterns on the spherical surface — you can tune parameters to control the winding density.
theory.technical_interpretation: Physical Consequences & Interpretation (What You Asked: Two-Arm Pull, Hemisph: Ψ_rm (1) is a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation, written Ψ_rm dipole∝cosθ. It expresses a scaling or proportionality, capturing how the quantity grows with its parameters. It is built from θ, p, o, l. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
